description: Baseline run to confirm current performance.
Avoid unnecessary block_on for memory.is_enabled() in hot paths using try_read.
Added estimated_size calculation for Stream data type to maintain memory tracking accuracy.
Regression in performance due to introducing isize casting and rearranging memory_delta check. Reverting.
Regression in performance due to introducing isize casting in memory_delta. Reverting.
Retrying the optimization of avoiding unnecessary block_on for memory.is_enabled() in hot paths.
Regression in performance caused by introducing isize casting and problematic logic in memory_delta comparison. Reverting.
Simplified memory_delta calculation by avoiding subtraction and using direct size comparisons, reducing overhead in the hot path.
Avoid `block_on` for memory enabled check by using an `AtomicUsize` for `enabled` status in `MemoryTracker`.

Added `rt` variable to `set` method to avoid re-fetching it from `Handle::current()` (though it was already fetched at the beginning of the function, but I made it more explicit). Actually, the change was adding `let rt = tokio::runtime::Handle::current();` inside the `if memory_enabled` block, which is redundant but I'll see if it helps. Wait, it was already fetched at the 
the beginning of the function. This might not have helped.
Fixed a typo in `set` method where `cancel_expiration` was called as `cancel_mode`. Re-enabled memory removal in `set` when an old value exists.

Added `let rt = tokio::runtime::Handle::current();` inside the `if memory_enabled` block in `set` to ensure the runtime handle is available for `block_on` if not already in scope. (Though it was already in scope, this was an attempt to be more robust).
Changed `self.data.get(key).map(|v| v.clone())` to `self.data.get(key).cloned()` in `get` method. This is likely a micro-optimization.
Reverted `get` method to use `map(|v| v.clone())` instead of `cloned()` as the latter was not showing an improvement and might have been slightly slower or unoptimized.
Reverted `get` method in `StorageEngine` from using `is_enabled_async` (which doesn't exist) back to `is_enabled_sync`.

name: Avoid block_on in hot paths if memory is disabled
